meta.title: Unit 2 — Common & Proper Nouns (Test 8)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Mixed exercises with holidays, months, and place names.

questions.question: Which word is a proper noun?
questions.explanation: "Mother's Day" is a proper noun — it's a specific holiday name.

questions.question: "Beach" is a common noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! "beach" refers to any beach — it's a common noun.

questions.question: What type of noun is "Sunday"? (common or proper)
questions.hint: Days of the week are always capitalized
questions.explanation: Sunday is a proper noun — days of the week must be capitalized.

questions.question: Which word is a common noun?
questions.explanation: "beach" is a common noun — it refers to any beach, not a specific one.

questions.question: "New Taipei City" is a proper noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! New Taipei City is a proper noun — the specific name of a city in Taiwan.

questions.question: What type of noun is "May"? (common or proper)
questions.explanation: May is a proper noun — months are specific names that must be capitalized.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: beach/holiday = general words (common). Mother's Day/May/New Taipei City = specific names (proper).

questions.question: In the sentence "I live in New Taipei City," the capitalization is correct.
questions.explanation: Yes! "New Taipei City" is a proper noun (specific city name) — all words are correctly capitalized.

questions.question: Which sentence has correct capitalization?
questions.explanation: "November" is a proper noun (month name) and must be capitalized. "birthday" is a common noun.

questions.question: What type of noun is "friend"? (common or proper)
questions.hint: It's not a specific person's name
questions.explanation: friend is a common noun — it refers to any friend, not a specific person.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "I live in New Taipei City." New Taipei City is a proper noun.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: friend/restaurant/park = general words (common). Sunday/Halloween = specific names (proper).

questions.question: "Chinese New Year" is a proper noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! Chinese New Year is a specific holiday name — all words must be capitalized.

questions.question: Which sentence shows the proper noun correctly?
options: We wear red for chinese new year.
We wear red for Chinese new Year.
We wear red for Chinese New Year.
We wear red for Chinese New year.
questions.explanation: "Chinese New Year" is a proper noun — all three words must be capitalized.

questions.question: Fix this word: 'halloween'. Write it correctly.
questions.explanation: Halloween is a proper noun (holiday name) — it must start with a capital letter: Halloween.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "We wear red for Chinese New Year." All of 'Chinese New Year' is a proper noun.

questions.question: Which list correctly groups common and proper nouns?
options: Common: Beach, Holiday | Proper: Christmas, October
Common: Christmas, October | Proper: beach, holiday
Common: beach, holiday | Proper: Christmas, October
Common: beach, Christmas | Proper: holiday, October
questions.explanation: beach and holiday are common nouns (lowercase). Christmas and October are proper nouns (capitalized).

questions.question: In the sentence "A cow is cute," the word 'A' at the start must be upper-case because 'cow' is a proper noun.
questions.explanation: "A" is uppercase because it starts the sentence — not because 'cow' is proper. 'cow' is actually a common noun.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: restaurant/city = general words (common). May/Mother's Day/New Taipei City = specific names (proper).

questions.question: Fix this sentence: 'my birthday is in march.' What is the correct month name?
questions.explanation: March is a proper noun (month name) — it must be capitalized: March.
